title: M-01: Config Schema & Validation — Implement Pydantic schemas with 3-layer validation
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DataConfig, MDPConfig, ActionSpec, RewardWeights, AgentConfig, ExperimentConfig defined as Pydantic models
- [ ] YAML → Pydantic loading pipeline implemented
- [ ] 3-layer validation: schema → registry → dummy step
- [ ] Invalid configs rejected with clear error messages
- [ ] Example config files for synthetic data experiments
- [ ] `uv run ty check` passes with concrete types

## Definition of Done
All config types validated from YAML; 3-layer validation catches wiring errors before experiment starts.

## Source
subphase_1a_data_layer.md, subphase_1b_mdp_environment.md, subphase_1d_agent_library.md, subphase_1e_experiment_runner.md

title: M-02: StateView & Environment — Implement StateView dataclass and Environment with step/reset API
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StateView dataclass with features dict, user_id, timestamp, metadata
- [ ] StateView.from_dataset(dataset, user_idx, t) classmethod
- [ ] Environment class with step(state, action) → (StateView, float, bool)
- [ ] Environment.reset() → StateView
- [ ] Multi-timescale reward accumulator (immediate + 3-week delayed)
- [ ] Episode termination logic
- [ ] Environment tests pass

## Definition of Done
Environment.step() returns correctly typed StateView; reward includes both components at correct epochs.

## Source
subphase_1b_mdp_environment.md, code_design.md, initial_design.tex §3

title: M-03: Transition & Reward Models — Implement RuleBasedTransition and CompoundReward
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RuleBasedTransition implements behavioural response model
- [ ] CompoundReward implements multi-timescale reward
- [ ] Burden accumulation/decay logic
- [ ] Configurable reward weights (α, β, λ, η) and action penalties
- [ ] Transition and reward tests pass

## Definition of Done
Transition model produces state changes; burden accumulates/decays correctly; compound reward computes both components.

## Source
subphase_1b_mdp_environment.md, initial_design.tex §3.2, §3.3

title: M-04: User Simulation Engine — Implement UserProfile with 4 archetypes
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] UserProfile Pydantic schema
- [ ] RuleBasedResponse with archetype-specific responses
- [ ] 4 archetypes: goal-driven, social responder, resistant, stable maintainer
- [ ] Parameter ranges for each archetype
- [ ] Response model tests pass

## Definition of Done
4 archetypes produce distinct response patterns; burden threshold triggers response decay.

## Source
subphase_1c_user_simulation.md, initial_design.tex §3.2

title: M-05: Thompson Sampling Agent — Implement full TS algorithm
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ThompsonSamplingAgent with Gaussian/Beta TS
- [ ] Configurable prior parameters and exploration rate
- [ ] Agent.select_action(state) returns valid action
- [ ] Agent.update() updates posterior
- [ ] Regret decreases on bandit problem
- [ ] Agent tests pass

## Definition of Done
Agent implements full TS algorithm; regret decreases ≥20% over 1000 episodes.

## Source
subphase_1d_agent_library.md

title: M-06: Experiment Runner & CLI — Implement end-to-end experiment execution
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ExperimentFactory.build(config) → Experiment
- [ ] Experiment.run() → ExperimentResult
- [ ] CLI with --config flag
- [ ] ExperimentResult with config snapshot, agent results, seeds
- [ ] Results output: console table + CSV/JSON
- [ ] Reproducibility: same config + seed → identical results
- [ ] Integration tests pass

## Definition of Done
`uv run rl-health-interventions --config experiment.yml` runs end-to-end; results saved.

## Source
subphase_1e_experiment_runner.md, code_design.md

title: M-07: Synthetic Data Pipeline — Complete multi-feature synthetic data generation
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SyntheticDataGenerator produces steps, HR, sleep, sedentary time
- [ ] Population statistics from NHANES, All of Us, UK Biobank
- [ ] Distribution parameters configurable
- [ ] Synthetic data validation tests
- [ ] Example synthetic dataset

## Definition of Done
Synthetic data statistical properties within 10% of published population stats.

## Source
subphase_1a_data_layer.md, initial_design.tex §5

title: M-08: Evaluation Framework — Define baselines, metrics, and statistical analysis
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Baseline agents: random, fixed, rule-based
- [ ] Metrics: regret, reward, adherence, sustained change
- [ ] Statistical analysis: bootstrap CIs, effect sizes
- [ ] Power analysis document
- [ ] Evaluation tests pass

## Definition of Done
≥3 baselines implemented; statistical power ≥80% for 10% improvement detection.

## Source
Derived from Phase 1 gap analysis

title: M-09: Documentation & Examples — Complete API docs and example configs
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sphinx/mkdocs setup with auto-generated API docs
- [ ] 3 example experiment configs
- [ ] README with architecture diagram and working quickstart
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md updated
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md created

## Definition of Done
API docs cover 100% of public APIs; 3 example configs tested; README quickstart works.

## Source
Derived from Phase 2 doc-alignment audit

title: M-10: Safety & Ethics Review — Add safety constraints and privacy documentation
body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hard safety constraints: max intervention frequency, min recovery, burden limits
- [ ] Safety violation logging
- [ ] Privacy documentation: GDPR/HIPAA compliance
- [ ] Ethics section in initial_design.tex
- [ ] Safety tests pass

## Definition of Done
100% of safety constraints enforced; privacy docs cover all Phase 2 datasets.

## Source
Derived from Phase 1 gap analysis

title: RISK-01: Reward hacking — agent exploits reward function without genuine behaviour change
body:
## Risk Description
Agent may discover unintended strategies to maximise reward (e.g., always intervene to get step-change reward, ignoring burden penalty).

## Affected Milestones
M-03, M-05, M-08

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Analyse reward landscape for exploitable patterns
- [ ] Add hard constraints on intervention frequency
- [ ] Monitor reward distribution during training for anomalies
- [ ] Test agents against adversarial reward scenarios
- [ ] Document known reward hacking vectors

## Severity
Likelihood: High | Impact: High

title: RISK-02: Distribution shift — synthetic data doesn't match real user behaviour
body:
## Risk Description
Rule-based simulator produces synthetic trajectories that differ systematically from real user behaviour, invalidating agent evaluation.

## Affected Milestones
M-04, M-07, M-08

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Validate synthetic data statistics against published population data
- [ ] Implement domain randomisation for robustness testing
- [ ] Plan Phase 2 calibration with HeartSteps MRT data
- [ ] Document sim-to-real gap as known limitation
- [ ] Sensitivity analysis on archetype parameters

## Severity
Likelihood: High | Impact: High

title: RISK-03: Safety violations — intervention policy causes user harm or excessive burden
body:
## Risk Description
RL agent may recommend interventions that exceed safe frequency or ignore user recovery needs, causing notification fatigue or adverse effects.

## Affected Milestones
M-03, M-04, M-10

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Implement hard burden threshold with intervention blocking
- [ ] Add maximum intervention frequency constraint
- [ ] Log all safety violations with timestamps
- [ ] Test policy under adversarial user profiles
- [ ] Document safety constraints in design doc

## Severity
Likelihood: Medium | Impact: Critical

title: RISK-04: Data leakage — training data includes future information
body:
## Risk Description
Feature engineering or data loading may inadvertently include future state information, giving agents unfair advantage.

## Affected Milestones
M-01, M-02, M-07

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Audit feature pipeline for temporal leakage
- [ ] Implement strict train/test split by time
- [ ] Add tests verifying no future information access
- [ ] Document data preprocessing pipeline with timestamps
- [ ] Code review for data loading logic

## Severity
Likelihood: Medium | Impact: High

title: RISK-05: Clinical validity failures — agent performance doesn't translate to clinical outcomes
body:
## Risk Description
Agent may achieve high reward/regret metrics without producing clinically meaningful behaviour change (e.g., 100 extra steps/day is statistically significant but clinically irrelevant).

## Affected Milestones
M-08, M-10

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Define clinically meaningful thresholds for step changes
- [ ] Consult clinical literature for minimal important difference
- [ ] Add clinical outcome metrics alongside ML metrics
- [ ] Validate against published intervention effect sizes
- [ ] Document clinical relevance criteria

## Severity
Likelihood: High | Impact: High

title: RISK-06: Config schema rigidity — schema too restrictive for extensibility
body:
## Risk Description
Pydantic schemas may be too strict, preventing researchers from adding custom fields or extending the framework.

## Affected Milestones
M-01

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Use Pydantic `extra="allow"` for forward compatibility
- [ ] Document extension points clearly
- [ ] Test schema with non-standard configs
- [ ] Gather feedback from external users early

## Severity
Likelihood: Medium | Impact: Medium

title: RISK-07: Credit assignment failure — delayed reward not properly attributed
body:
## Risk Description
3-week delayed body measure reward may not be correctly attributed to actions taken weeks earlier, causing poor learning.

## Affected Milestones
M-02, M-03

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Implement eligibility traces or temporal difference methods
- [ ] Test credit assignment on simple scenarios
- [ ] Compare sparse vs decaying reward formulations
- [ ] Document credit assignment limitations

## Severity
Likelihood: High | Impact: Medium

title: RISK-08: Missing dataset loaders — allofus_fitbit and stepcountjitai not loadable
body:
## Risk Description
Two critical dataset configs exist but no loader functions, blocking Phase 2 validation.

## Affected Milestones
M-07

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Implement load_allofus_fitbit() with BigQuery/cloud access
- [ ] Implement load_stepcountjitai() with special download logic
- [ ] Document access requirements for each dataset
- [ ] Test loaders with sample data

## Severity
Likelihood: High | Impact: Medium

title: RISK-09: Archetype heterogeneity — 4 discrete archetypes don't capture continuous variation
body:
## Risk Description
Real users exist on a spectrum; 4 discrete archetypes may not capture the diversity of behavioural responses.

## Affected Milestones
M-04

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Document as known limitation
- [ ] Plan continuous parameter sampling for Phase 2
- [ ] Test with mixed-archetype populations
- [ ] Validate archetype assignments against real data

## Severity
Likelihood: High | Impact: Medium

title: RISK-10: Partial observability — MDP assumes full state observability but sensors have noise/missing data
body:
## Risk Description
Real wearable sensors have missing data, measurement noise, and reporting delays. Full observability assumption may not hold.

## Affected Milestones
M-02, M-04

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Implement observation function O(o|s) for POMDP framing
- [ ] Add sensor noise and missing data simulation
- [ ] Test agents under partial observability
- [ ] Document observability assumptions

## Severity
Likelihood: High | Impact: Medium

title: RISK-11: Insufficient evaluation metrics — ML metrics don't capture intervention effectiveness
body:
## Risk Description
Regret and reward may not capture clinically meaningful outcomes like sustained behaviour change or user engagement.

## Affected Milestones
M-08

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Add adherence rate metric
- [ ] Add sustained behaviour change metric (3+ months)
- [ ] Add user dropout/fatigue rate
- [ ] Consult clinical literature for relevant outcomes

## Severity
Likelihood: Medium | Impact: High

title: RISK-12: Performance bottlenecks — simulation too slow for large-scale experiments
body:
## Risk Description
Environment step loop may be too slow for experiments with 1000+ users × 365 days × multiple agents.

## Affected Milestones
M-06

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Profile simulation loop early
- [ ] Use numpy vectorisation where possible
- [ ] Implement parallel experiment execution
- [ ] Document performance benchmarks

## Severity
Likelihood: Medium | Impact: Medium

title: RISK-13: Dependency conflicts — external package version incompatibilities
body:
## Risk Description
pyproject.toml has 9 runtime dependencies; version conflicts may arise during installation or updates.

## Affected Milestones
M-01

## Mitigation Steps
- [ ] Pin dependency versions in uv.lock
- [ ] Test installation on clean environment
- [ ] Document minimum Python version (3.11+)
- [ ] Monitor for breaking changes in dependencies

## Severity
Likelihood: Low | Impact: Medium

title: DOC: subphase_1a through 1e — Status fields say 'Not started' but implementations exist
body:
## What is wrong
All 5 subphase files in docs/code/ say 'Status: [ ] Not started' when implementations exist for 1A (partial), 1B (partial), and 1D (partial).

## What correct state looks like
Each subphase file accurately reflects implementation status:
- 1A: [~] In progress — data layer partially implemented
- 1B: [~] In progress — ABC stubs done; Environment missing
- 1C: [ ] Not started — accurate
- 1D: [~] In progress — Agent ABC + TS stub done
- 1E: [ ] Not started — accurate

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 subphase files have accurate status fields
- [ ] Gate checklist items reflect actual implementation state
- [ ] README milestone diagram updated to match
- [ ] phase_1_execution_plan.md dependency graph updated

title: DOC: code_design.md — StateView, Environment, ExperimentConfig interfaces documented but not implemented
body:
## What is wrong
code_design.md defines StateView, Environment, and ExperimentConfig in detail. None exist in code. All component interfaces use `Any` type annotations.

## What correct state looks like
Either (a) implement all interfaces as documented, or (b) update design docs to reflect deferral with rationale.

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StateView dataclass implemented OR design doc updated with deferral rationale
- [ ] Environment class implemented OR design doc updated
- [ ] ExperimentConfig schema implemented OR design doc updated
- [ ] All ABC base classes updated from `Any` to concrete types
- [ ] `uv run ty check` passes with concrete types

title: DOC: README.md — Quickstart promises working system that doesn't exist
body:
## What is wrong
README says `uv run rl-health-interventions` runs the system. It actually prints 'Hello from rl-health-interventions!' and exits. No experiment runs.

## What correct state looks like
README quickstart either (a) runs a minimal working experiment, or (b) explicitly states 'Phase 1 implementation in progress' and points to design docs.

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] experiments/demo.yml exists with documented fields
- [ ] README quickstart either works OR clearly states implementation status
- [ ] Config schema documented
- [ ] New contributor can understand what the system will do

title: DOC: config/datasets/ — allofus_fitbit.yaml and stepcountjitai.yaml have no matching loaders
body:
## What is wrong
2 dataset configs exist but no corresponding loader functions in loaders.py. Users cannot load these datasets.

## What correct state looks like
Either (a) implement load_allofus_fitbit() and load_stepcountjitai(), or (b) remove configs and document why.

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All configs have matching loaders OR configs removed with documentation
- [ ] load_all() function handles missing loaders gracefully
- [ ] Dataset access requirements documented

title: DOC: API documentation — No Sphinx/mkdocs setup, minimal docstrings on ABCs
body:
## What is wrong
No API documentation exists. No Sphinx/mkdocs setup. ABC base classes have minimal docstrings. Contributors must read source code.

## What correct state looks like
Sphinx or mkdocs setup with auto-generated API docs. All public classes and functions have docstrings.

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sphinx or mkdocs configuration added
- [ ] Docstrings on all public classes and functions
- [ ] API docs build without errors
- [ ] Docs hosted or buildable locally

title: DOC: codebase_plan.md — Done Criteria lists 5 deliverables, none met
body:
## What is wrong
codebase_plan.md lists 5 done criteria (working CLI, config schema documented, README enables running, config stable across schemas, dataset feasibility report). None are fully met.

## What correct state looks like
Done criteria accurately reflect current state. Unmet criteria have clear tracking.

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each done criterion has accurate status (met/partial/not met)
- [ ] Unmet criteria have clear path to completion
- [ ] Criteria linked to roadmap milestones

title: DOC: initial_design.tex — Decision Log has open questions that may be resolved
body:
## What is wrong
Decision Log lists 'Is MDP formalisation reasonable? — Awaiting supervisor approval' as Open. Code implements the MDP structure, suggesting approval may have been given.

## What correct state looks like
Decision Log accurately reflects current decision status. Resolved questions marked as confirmed.

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Decision Log entries have accurate status
- [ ] Resolved questions marked as confirmed
- [ ] Open questions have clear owners and timelines

title: DOC: No example experiment configs exist in repo
body:
## What is wrong
README references `--config experiment.yml` but no example configs exist. Users cannot learn config schema from examples.

## What correct state looks like
At least 3 example configs demonstrating different use cases.

## PR Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] experiments/synthetic_baseline.yml
- [ ] experiments/multi_agent_comparison.yml
- [ ] experiments/sensitivity_analysis.yml
- [ ] Each config tested and working
- [ ] Config fields documented

title: IMPL: src/rl_health_interventions/data/synthetic.py — Implement multi-feature synthetic data generation
body:
## What must be implemented
SyntheticDataGenerator currently generates only 'steps' feature. Must generate: steps, heart_rate, sleep_hours, sedentary_min, with realistic distributions from published population statistics.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Output includes steps, HR, sleep, sedentary features
- [ ] Step counts non-negative, mean ~8000, std ~2000
- [ ] Heart rate in physiological range (40-200 bpm)
- [ ] Sleep hours realistic (4-12 hours)
- [ ] No NaN values in output
- [ ] Configurable distribution parameters
- [ ] Reproducible with seed

title: IMPL: src/rl_health_interventions/data/feature_pipeline.py — Implement config-driven feature engineering
body:
## What must be implemented
FeaturePipeline is currently a stub that accepts config but ignores it. Must implement: from_config() that builds Polars lazy computation graph, transform() that applies aggregations, rolling windows, normalisation.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] from_config() builds computation graph from config
- [ ] transform() applies normalisation, aggregation, derived features
- [ ] Polars lazy API used for efficiency
- [ ] Output is numpy arrays for simulation loop
- [ ] Config-driven column mapping works
- [ ] Tests for each transform type

title: IMPL: src/rl_health_interventions/transitions/rule_based.py — Implement behavioural response model
body:
## What must be implemented
RuleBasedTransition.transition() currently returns state unchanged. Must implement: state change based on action + user profile, burden accumulation/decay, archetype-specific responses.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] transition() produces different state changes for different actions
- [ ] Burden accumulates on intervention, decays on no-action
- [ ] Burden threshold triggers response decay
- [ ] 4 archetypes produce distinct response patterns
- [ ] Configurable parameters for each archetype
- [ ] Tests for each archetype response direction

title: IMPL: src/rl_health_interventions/rewards/compound.py — Implement multi-timescale reward
body:
## What must be implemented
CompoundReward.reward() currently returns (0.0, False). Must implement: immediate reward (α·Δactivity - β·reward_penalty + λ·goal_progress), delayed reward (η·BM_improvement every 21 days), burden tracking.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Immediate reward computed correctly with configurable weights
- [ ] Delayed reward non-zero only at 21-day boundaries
- [ ] Total reward = immediate + delayed at boundary epochs
- [ ] Action penalties applied correctly
- [ ] Goal progress calculation defined and tested
- [ ] Tests for reward components

title: IMPL: src/rl_health_interventions/agents/thompson_sampling.py — Implement full Thompson Sampling algorithm
body:
## What must be implemented
ThompsonSamplingAgent currently returns action 0 always. Must implement: posterior sampling, action selection based on sampled values, posterior updates after each step, configurable priors.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] select_action() samples from posterior and returns action
- [ ] update() updates posterior with observed reward
- [ ] Regret decreases over time on bandit problem
- [ ] Configurable prior parameters
- [ ] Handles exploration-exploitation tradeoff
- [ ] Tests for action distribution, posterior updates, regret

title: IMPL: src/rl_health_interventions/simulation/rule_based.py — Implement archetype-specific response model
body:
## What must be implemented
RuleBasedResponse.response() currently returns 0.0. Must implement: archetype-specific response magnitudes, burden threshold effects, parameterised response functions.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] response() returns different values for different archetypes
- [ ] Goal-driven responds to reminders/feedback
- [ ] Social responder responds to motivational prompts
- [ ] Resistant shows flat response
- [ ] Stable maintainer shows low marginal gain
- [ ] Burden threshold reduces response probability
- [ ] Tests for each archetype

title: IMPL: src/rl_health_interventions/__main__.py — Implement CLI with config-driven experiment execution
body:
## What must be implemented
__main__.py currently prints 'Hello' and exits. Must implement: CLI argument parsing (--config flag), config loading, ExperimentFactory invocation, results output.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] CLI accepts --config argument
- [ ] Config loaded and validated
- [ ] Experiment runs end-to-end
- [ ] Results output to console and file
- [ ] Config snapshot saved with results
- [ ] Seed management for reproducibility
- [ ] Tests for CLI argument parsing

title: IMPL: NEW — src/rl_health_interventions/state.py — Implement StateView dataclass
body:
## What must be implemented
StateView dataclass as defined in code_design.md: features dict, user_id, timestamp, metadata, from_dataset() classmethod.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] StateView is a dataclass with correct fields
- [ ] from_dataset(dataset, user_idx, t) extracts correct slice
- [ ] Features dict contains all state variables
- [ ] Metadata includes week number, body measure flag
- [ ] Type hints are concrete (not Any)
- [ ] Tests for StateView creation and slicing

title: IMPL: NEW — src/rl_health_interventions/environment.py — Implement Environment with step/reset API
body:
## What must be implemented
Environment class with step(state, action) → (StateView, float, bool) and reset() → StateView. Multi-timescale reward accumulator. Episode termination logic.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] step() returns (StateView, float, bool) tuple
- [ ] reset() returns initial StateView
- [ ] Reward includes immediate + delayed components
- [ ] Delayed reward at correct 21-day boundaries
- [ ] Episode termination works (fixed-length or condition)
- [ ] Tests for step API contract, reward timing, episode boundaries

title: IMPL: NEW — src/rl_health_interventions/config/schemas.py — Implement all Pydantic config schemas
body:
## What must be implemented
DataConfig, MDPConfig, ActionSpec, RewardWeights, AgentConfig, ExperimentConfig as Pydantic models. 3-layer validation pipeline.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] All config types defined as Pydantic models
- [ ] YAML → Pydantic loading works
- [ ] Invalid configs raise ValidationError with clear messages
- [ ] 3-layer validation: schema → registry → dummy step
- [ ] Config defaults documented
- [ ] Tests for valid/invalid configs

title: IMPL: NEW — src/rl_health_interventions/simulation/user_profile.py — Implement UserProfile schema
body:
## What must be implemented
UserProfile Pydantic schema with archetype field (4 options) and parameters dict. Default parameter ranges for each archetype.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] UserProfile is a Pydantic model
- [ ] Archetype field accepts 4 values
- [ ] Parameters dict is validated
- [ ] Default parameters for each archetype
- [ ] Invalid archetypes rejected
- [ ] Tests for profile creation and validation

title: IMPL: NEW — src/rl_health_interventions/experiment/ — Implement ExperimentFactory and Experiment
body:
## What must be implemented
ExperimentFactory.build(config) → Experiment. Experiment.run() → ExperimentResult. Config-driven component instantiation. Results output.

## Test Criteria
- [ ] ExperimentFactory.build() instantiates all components from config
- [ ] Experiment.run() executes full simulation loop
- [ ] ExperimentResult contains config snapshot, agent results, seeds
- [ ] Results output to console and file
- [ ] Same config + seed → identical results
- [ ] Integration tests for end-to-end execution
